docs.title: Deep Learning Tutorial
docs.text:
Welcome to the complete Deep Learning tutorial.

This website explains:

  • Deep Learning concepts
  • Neural Networks
  • CNNs
  • Transformers
  • Model Training
  • TensorFlow
  • PyTorch

docs.title: Course Roadmap
docs.text:
Machine Learning
        |
        ↓
Deep Learning
        |
        ↓
Neural Networks
        |
        ↓
Advanced AI

docs.title: What is Deep Learning?
docs.text:
Deep Learning is a specialized branch of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) that enables computers to automatically learn complex patterns from large amounts of data.

It uses artificial neural networks with multiple layers to simulate the way humans process information.

The main idea behind deep learning is that a computer system can learn from examples instead of being explicitly programmed with rules.

For example, instead of programming thousands of rules to identify a cat in an image, a deep learning model analyzes thousands of cat and non-cat images and learns the important features automatically.

docs.title: Artificial Intelligence (AI)
docs.text:
Artificial Intelligence is the broad field of computer science that focuses on creating machines capable of performing tasks that normally require human intelligence.

These tasks include:

  • Learning
  • Reasoning
  • Decision making
  • Problem solving
  • Understanding language
  • Recognizing patterns

Examples of AI applications:

  • Virtual assistants
  • Self-driving vehicles
  • Recommendation systems
  • Chatbots
  • Image recognition

docs.title: Machine Learning
docs.text:
Machine Learning is a subset of Artificial Intelligence where computers learn from data.

Instead of writing a fixed program, we provide examples and allow the system to discover patterns.

Example:

A traditional program:

Input
  |
Rules written by humans
  |
Output

Machine Learning:

Input Data
     |
Learning Algorithm
     |
Trained Model
     |
Prediction

docs.title: Deep Learning vs Machine Learning
docs.text: Machine Learning Deep Learning Requires manual feature extraction Automatically learns features Works with smaller datasets Requires large datasets Uses algorithms like Random Forest and SVM Uses neural networks Less computational power needed Requires GPUs/TPUs Good for structured data Excellent for unstructured data

docs.title: Why Deep Learning Became Popular?
docs.text: Deep learning became successful because of three major factors:

docs.title: 1. Large Amounts of Data
docs.text:
Modern systems generate huge datasets:

  • Images
  • Videos
  • Text
  • Sensor measurements
  • Satellite data

Deep learning models improve as they see more examples.

docs.title: 2. Powerful Hardware
docs.text:
Graphics Processing Units (GPUs) allow neural networks to perform millions of calculations efficiently.

Modern deep learning uses:

  • GPUs
  • TPUs
  • Cloud computing

docs.title: 3. Improved Algorithms
docs.text:
New architectures and optimization methods made deep learning faster and more accurate.

Examples:

  • CNNs
  • Transformers
  • Attention mechanisms

docs.title: How Deep Learning Works
docs.text:
A deep learning model follows this general process:

Data Collection

      ↓

Data Preparation

      ↓

Neural Network Training

      ↓

Learning Patterns

      ↓

Prediction

docs.title: Example: Image Classification
docs.text:
Input:

Image of a dog

The neural network learns:

Pixels

 ↓

Edges

 ↓

Shapes

 ↓

Body parts

 ↓

Dog

The model does not receive rules about dogs.

It discovers these patterns from examples.

docs.title: Computer Vision
docs.text:
Deep learning allows computers to understand images and videos.

Applications:

  • Face recognition
  • Object detection
  • Medical image analysis
  • Satellite image classification

docs.title: Natural Language Processing (NLP)
docs.text:
Deep learning enables computers to understand human language.

Applications:

  • Chatbots
  • Translation
  • Text summarization
  • Question answering

docs.title: Speech Recognition
docs.text:
Used for:

  • Voice assistants
  • Automatic transcription
  • Voice commands

docs.title: Autonomous Systems
docs.text:
Examples:

  • Self-driving cars
  • Robots
  • Drones

docs.title: Earth Observation and GeoAI
docs.text:
Deep learning is widely used in geospatial science.

Applications:

  • Land cover mapping
  • Flood detection
  • Deforestation monitoring
  • Urban growth analysis
  • Climate modeling

docs.title: Automatic Feature Learning
docs.text:
Traditional methods require humans to identify important features.

Deep learning learns features automatically.

docs.title: High Accuracy
docs.text: With enough data, deep learning can achieve excellent performance.

docs.title: Handles Complex Data
docs.text:
Deep learning works well with:

  • Images
  • Text
  • Audio
  • Video

docs.title: Requires Large Data
docs.text: Deep learning usually needs thousands or millions of examples.

docs.title: Computational Cost
docs.text:
Training large models requires:

  • GPUs
  • High memory
  • Long training time

docs.title: Lack of Explainability
docs.text: Deep learning models are often considered "black boxes" because understanding their decisions can be difficult.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Deep Learning is a powerful approach that allows computers to learn complex patterns automatically.

Key ideas:

  • Neural networks are the foundation
  • Multiple layers create deep models
  • Models learn from data
  • Training adjusts internal parameters
  • Deep learning powers modern AI systems

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
A Neural Network is a computational model inspired by the structure and function of the human brain.

It consists of interconnected processing units called neurons that work together to learn patterns from data.

Neural networks are the fundamental building blocks of deep learning.

docs.title: Basic Structure
docs.text:
A simple neural network contains three main parts:

Input Layer
      |
      ↓
Hidden Layer(s)
      |
      ↓
Output Layer

docs.title: Input Layer
docs.text:
The input layer receives the original data.

Examples:

Image:

Pixel values

Weather prediction:

Temperature
Humidity
Wind speed

Text processing:

Word embeddings

The input layer passes information to the next layers.

docs.title: Hidden Layers
docs.text:
Hidden layers perform the actual learning process.

Each hidden layer transforms the input into more meaningful representations.

Example: Image Recognition

First layer:

Detect edges

Second layer:

Detect shapes

Third layer:

Detect objects

The deeper the network, the more complex patterns it can learn.

docs.title: Output Layer
docs.text:
The output layer produces the final result.

Examples:

Classification:

Cat = 95%
Dog = 5%

Regression:

Predicted temperature = 28°C

docs.title: Neurons
docs.text:
A neuron is the smallest processing unit of a neural network.

A neuron receives inputs, applies weights and bias, and produces an output.

Mathematical representation:

\[ y = f(wx+b) \]

Where:

Symbol Meaning x Input w Weight b Bias f Activation function y Output

docs.title: Weights
docs.text:
Weights determine how important an input is.

During training, the model adjusts weights to improve predictions.

Example:

If temperature strongly affects rainfall prediction:

Temperature weight = high

If another variable is less important:

Variable weight = low

docs.title: Bias
docs.text:
Bias allows neurons to adjust their output independently from the input.

It helps the model fit data better.

docs.title: Activation Function
docs.text:
An activation function decides whether a neuron should activate.

It introduces non-linearity into the network.

Without activation functions:

Deep network behaves like a simple linear model

Common activation functions:

  • ReLU
  • Sigmoid
  • Tanh
  • Softmax

docs.title: Forward Propagation
docs.text:
Forward propagation is the process of passing information from input to output.

Steps:

  1. Input data enters network
  2. Each neuron calculates output
  3. Prediction is generated

Example:

Input Image

      ↓

Neural Network

      ↓

Prediction

      ↓

Cat

docs.title: Loss Function
docs.text:
The loss function measures how wrong the prediction is.

Example:

Actual value:

Cat

Prediction:

Dog

The loss will be high.

Training tries to minimize this loss.

docs.title: Backpropagation
docs.text:
Backpropagation is the learning mechanism of neural networks.

The model:

  1. Makes a prediction
  2. Calculates error
  3. Sends error backward
  4. Updates weights

Process:

Prediction

     ↓

Loss Calculation

     ↓

Error

     ↓

Weight Update

docs.title: Training Neural Networks
docs.text:
Training involves repeatedly:

  • Feeding data
  • Making predictions
  • Calculating errors
  • Updating weights

This process happens many times until the model improves.

docs.title: Feed Forward Neural Network
docs.text:
Information moves only forward.

Applications:

  • Classification
  • Regression

docs.title: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)
docs.text:
Designed for images.

Applications:

  • Object detection
  • Remote sensing

docs.title: Recurrent Neural Network (RNN)
docs.text:
Designed for sequential data.

Applications:

  • Time series
  • Text

docs.title: Transformer Networks
docs.text:
Modern architecture for language and vision.

Applications:

  • ChatGPT
  • Translation

docs.title: Applications
docs.text:
Neural networks are used in:

  • Image recognition
  • Weather prediction
  • Fraud detection
  • Medical diagnosis
  • Autonomous vehicles
  • Natural language processing

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Neural networks learn patterns by connecting many artificial neurons.

Important concepts:

  • Layers
  • Neurons
  • Weights
  • Bias
  • Activation functions
  • Forward propagation
  • Backpropagation
  • Training
